title: Review thread on src/services/code-context-gatherer.ts:null
content:
_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_

**Shell command in getFileTree is less risky but should also use spawn.**

While `getFileTree()` doesn't interpolate user input, using `execSync` with a shell command string is a less safe pattern. Consider using `spawnSync` with an array of arguments for consistency and defense-in-depth.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/services/code-context-gatherer.ts` around lines 140 - 155, Replace the
execSync usage in getFileTree with spawnSync to avoid invoking a shell string:
call spawnSync (from child_process) with the command "find" and an args array
['.', '-type', 'f', '(', '-name', '*.ts', '-o', '-name', '*.js', '-o', '-name',
'*.tsx', '-o', '-name', '*.jsx', '-o', '-name', '*.py', '-o', '-name', '*.go',
')', '-not', '-path', '*/node_modules/*', '-not', '-path', '*/dist/*', '-not',
'-path', '*/build/*', '-not', '-path', '*/.git/*'] and pipe the output through
head by running a second spawnSync for "head" with ['-200'] or by reading and
truncating the output programmatically; ensure you set cwd to this.workingDir,
encoding to 'utf-8', stdio appropriate for capturing stderr/stdout, timeout
preserved, check spawnSync.status and stderr and return the same "File
tree:\n${result}" or the fallback string on error, and remove any shell: true
usage so the function remains secure and consistent.
```

</details>

title: Review thread on src/services/openai-service.ts:241
content:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Fail fast here instead of prompting for config from the non-interactive rewrite path.**

Line 94 calls `ensureInitialized()` before the method’s `try`. In this class, that can invoke `promptForMissingConfig('openaiApiKey')`, so `--rewrite-prompt` can unexpectedly become interactive inside `executeNonInteractiveWorkflow`, and initialization failures bypass the `Prompt rewrite failed:` wrapper. Check for a configured key first and keep initialization inside the guarded block.  


<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 async rewritePrompt(ticket: string): Promise<string> {
-  await this.ensureInitialized();
-
   const systemPrompt = `You are a prompt optimizer for coding agents. Transform verbose development tickets into clean, structured prompts for Claude Code.
 ...
 Output ONLY the rewritten prompt. No preamble, no explanation.`;
 
   try {
+    const config = this.configManager.getConfig();
+    if (!config?.openaiApiKey) {
+      throw new Error('OpenAI API key is required when prompt rewriting is enabled');
+    }
+
+    await this.ensureInitialized();
     if (!this.openai) throw new Error('OpenAI client not initialized');
 
     const response = await this.openai.chat.completions.create({
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/services/openai-service.ts` around lines 93 - 149, The rewritePrompt
method calls ensureInitialized() before the try block which may invoke
promptForMissingConfig('openaiApiKey') and make a non-interactive path
interactive; change rewritePrompt (and its callers like
executeNonInteractiveWorkflow) to first check for an already-configured OpenAI
key/client (e.g., inspect this.openai or this.config.openaiApiKey) and throw a
fast error if missing, then call ensureInitialized() inside the try so
initialization and any thrown errors are caught and wrapped by the existing
catch; reference rewritePrompt, ensureInitialized, promptForMissingConfig and
executeNonInteractiveWorkflow when locating the code to update.
```

</details>

title: Review thread on src/services/openai-service.ts:null
content:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Bound the ticket size before sending it to the model.**

Lines 134-139 forward the full ticket unbounded, even though this feature explicitly targets verbose prompts. Large issue dumps or pasted logs can hit model limits and force the workflow down the fallback path. Add the same kind of truncation guard you already use in `generatePullRequestDescription()`, ideally preserving both the head and tail of the input.  


<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
   try {
     if (!this.openai) throw new Error('OpenAI client not initialized');
+
+    const MAX_TICKET_LENGTH = 30000;
+    const promptTicket =
+      ticket.length > MAX_TICKET_LENGTH
+        ? `${ticket.slice(0, MAX_TICKET_LENGTH / 2)}\n\n... (ticket truncated - ${ticket.length} total characters) ...\n\n${ticket.slice(-MAX_TICKET_LENGTH / 2)}`
+        : ticket;
 
     const response = await this.openai.chat.completions.create({
       model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
       messages: [
           { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
-          { role: 'user', content: ticket }
+          { role: 'user', content: promptTicket }
       ],
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/services/openai-service.ts` around lines 134 - 140, The ticket string
sent to the model in openai.chat.completions.create (inside the method where
model 'gpt-4o-mini' is used) must be size-limited like
generatePullRequestDescription() does: implement or call a truncation helper to
cap the ticket length (e.g., N chars) and preserve the head and tail (ellipsis
in middle) before building messages so huge tickets or logs don't exceed model
limits; update the messages array to use the truncatedTicket variable instead of
the raw ticket and ensure max_tokens logic remains consistent with the truncated
input.
```

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

Adds prompt-rewriting support across the codebase. A new migration (id 14) adds an original_description TEXT column to the tasks table. The Task type and JobManager now include original_description, and JobManager exposes updateTaskOriginalDescription. OpenAIService gains rewritePrompt(ticket: string): Promise<string>. A new PromptRewriter class wraps that service. TaskExecutor and the CLI/main flow were updated to accept and propagate a rewritePrompt flag to optionally rewrite task descriptions before creation, preserving originals on success or falling back on failure.

## Poem

> 🐰 I munched a long prompt and gave it a squeeze,  
> I hopped through the words and pruned every breeze,  
> Tucked the old lines safe in a quiet lair,  
> Fed the new hush to tasks with a careful care,  
> Hop—now each task sings, neat and aware! 🎶

title: Review thread on src/database/migrations/014_add_original_description_column.ts:null
content:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Multi-statement migration lacks transactional safety.**

Per the migration runner in `src/database/migration.ts` (lines 25-27), statements are executed individually without transaction wrapping. If the second `ALTER TABLE` fails, the first column will remain, leaving the schema partially migrated.

Consider combining into a single transaction or documenting the partial-failure recovery procedure. However, since SQLite `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` rarely fails and this pattern is consistent with other migrations in the codebase, this is low risk.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/database/migrations/014_add_prompt_rewriting_columns.ts` around lines 6 -
9, This migration runs two separate ALTER TABLE statements in the up array which
can leave the DB partially migrated if the second fails; wrap the two ALTER
TABLEs in an explicit transaction so both succeed or both roll back (e.g.,
replace the two entries in the up array with a single SQL string that does BEGIN
TRANSACTION; ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN original_description TEXT; ALTER TABLE
tasks ADD COLUMN rewritten_description TEXT; COMMIT; or use the migration
runner's transaction helper if available), and mirror the same transactional
safety in any corresponding down/rollback steps.
```

</details>

title: Review thread on src/services/code-context-gatherer.ts:null
content:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Command injection vulnerability in shell execution.**

The `keyword` is derived from user-provided `questions` and is only escaped for double quotes before being interpolated into a shell command. This is insufficient protection against shell metacharacters.

An attacker-controlled question containing backticks, `$()`, or other shell constructs could execute arbitrary commands:
```
Questions: ["Where is `$(whoami > /tmp/pwned)` defined?"]
```


<details>
<summary>🔒 Proposed fix using spawn with explicit arguments</summary>

```diff
+import { spawnSync } from 'child_process';
-import { execSync } from 'child_process';

 private searchFiles(keywords: string[]): string[] {
   const matchingFiles = new Set<string>();

   for (const keyword of keywords) {
     try {
-      // Use grep to find files containing the keyword
-      const result = execSync(
-        `grep -rl --include="*.ts" --include="*.js" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.jsx" --include="*.py" --include="*.go" --include="*.rs" -m 5 "${keyword.replace(/"/g, '\\"')}" .`,
-        {
-          cwd: this.workingDir,
-          encoding: 'utf-8',
-          stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
-          timeout: 10000
-        }
-      );
+      // Use spawnSync to avoid shell injection
+      const result = spawnSync('grep', [
+        '-rl',
+        '--include=*.ts', '--include=*.js', '--include=*.tsx',
+        '--include=*.jsx', '--include=*.py', '--include=*.go', '--include=*.rs',
+        '-m', '5',
+        keyword,
+        '.'
+      ], {
+        cwd: this.workingDir,
+        encoding: 'utf-8',
+        timeout: 10000
+      });
+
+      if (result.status !== 0) continue;

-      const files = result.trim().split('\n').filter(f => f.length > 0);
+      const files = (result.stdout || '').trim().split('\n').filter(f => f.length > 0);
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/services/code-context-gatherer.ts` around lines 93 - 116, The loop over
keywords in code-context-gatherer.ts builds a shell string with user-controlled
keyword (inside the for (const keyword of keywords) block) causing command
injection; fix by invoking grep without a shell using child_process.spawnSync or
execFileSync (e.g., call 'grep' with args array instead of interpolating into a
template string), pass the keyword as a separate argument and add
--fixed-strings (or -F) so grep treats the keyword literally, keep cwd as
this.workingDir and existing options (encoding, timeout, stdio), and continue
using matchingFiles.add(join(this.workingDir, ...)) for results; remove the
custom double-quote escaping and the string interpolation to eliminate shell
interpretation.
```

</details>

title: Review thread on src/index.ts:null
content:
_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_

**Filter may miss single-dash flags.**

`nonFlagArgs` filters only args starting with `'--'`, but single-dash flags (e.g., `-c`, `-p`) would pass through. This works for the current use case since task descriptions shouldn't start with `-`, but could cause issues if new single-letter flags are added later.


<details>
<summary>♻️ More robust flag filtering</summary>

```diff
-    const nonFlagArgs = args.filter(arg => !arg.startsWith('--'));
+    const nonFlagArgs = args.filter(arg => !arg.startsWith('-'));
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
    const nonFlagArgs = args.filter(arg => !arg.startsWith('-'));
    const hasRewriteFlag = args.includes('--rewrite-prompt');

    if (nonFlagArgs.length > 0 && !recognizedCommands.includes(nonFlagArgs[0])) {
      // Treat the first non-flag argument as a task description
      const taskDescription = nonFlagArgs[0];

      // Create a NonInteractiveConfig with the task
      const config: NonInteractiveConfig = {
        tasks: [taskDescription],
        rewritePrompt: hasRewriteFlag
      };
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/index.ts` around lines 601 - 612, nonFlagArgs currently only filters out
double-dash flags (arg.startsWith('--')) so single-dash flags like -c or -p
would be treated as task descriptions; change the filter to exclude any arg that
starts with '-' (e.g., arg.startsWith('-')) when building nonFlagArgs so
single-letter flags are recognized too, keep the rest of the logic that checks
recognizedCommands and builds the NonInteractiveConfig with taskDescription and
hasRewriteFlag unchanged (ensure hasRewriteFlag still checks for
'--rewrite-prompt').
```

</details>

title: Review thread on thoughts/shared/specs/2026-03-05-prompt-rewriting.md:null
content:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Spec describes 3-step pipeline, but implementation only has 2 steps.**

The spec outlines:
1. Extract questions (implemented)
2. Objective research with code context (NOT implemented)
3. Rewrite prompt (implemented, but without research input)

The `conductObjectiveResearch()` method mentioned in line 186 is missing from `src/services/openai-service.ts`, and `src/services/prompt-rewriter.ts` (per context snippet 2) only calls `rewritePrompt(originalTicket)` without passing any research findings.

This means the "intent leakage prevention" benefit described in lines 173-176 is not realized—the current implementation is effectively a 1-step rewrite without objective codebase research.

<details>
<summary>🧰 Tools</summary>

<details>
<summary>🪛 markdownlint-cli2 (0.21.0)</summary>

[warning] 47-47: Fenced code blocks should be surrounded by blank lines

(MD031, blanks-around-fences)

</details>

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@thoughts/shared/specs/2026-03-05-prompt-rewriting.md` around lines 42 - 47,
The implementation is missing the objective research step: implement the
conductObjectiveResearch(questions: string[]) method in openai-service.ts to run
a fresh LLM/search over the codebase and return structured factual findings,
then update prompt-rewriter.ts to call
conductObjectiveResearch(extractedQuestions) before invoking rewritePrompt and
change rewritePrompt(originalTicket) to rewritePrompt(originalTicket,
researchFindings) so the rewriter receives the research results; ensure the
place that references conductObjectiveResearch() uses the new implementation and
adjust any tests or call sites to pass and consume the research findings.
```

</details>

In `@src/services/code-context-gatherer.ts`: - Around line 140-155: Replace the execSync usage in getFileTree with spawnSync to avoid invoking a shell string: call spawnSync (from child_process) with the command "find" and an args array ['.', '-type', 'f', '(', '-name', '*.ts', '-o', '-name', '*.js', '-o', '-name', '*.tsx', '-o', '-name', '*.jsx', '-o', '-name', '*.py', '-o', '-name', '*.go', ')', '-not', '-path', '*/node_modules/*', '-not', '-path', '*/dist/*', '-not', '-path', '*/build/*', '-not', '-path', '*/.git/*'] and pipe the output through head by running a second spawnSync for "head" with ['-200'] or by reading and truncating the output programmatically; ensure you set cwd to this.workingDir, encoding to 'utf-8', stdio appropriate for capturing stderr/stdout, timeout preserved, check spawnSync.status and stderr and return the same "File tree:\n${result}" or the fallback string on error, and remove any shell: true usage so the function remains secure and consistent. - Around line 93-116: The loop over keywords in code-context-gatherer.ts builds a shell string with user-controlled keyword (inside the for (const keyword of keywords) block) causing command injection; fix by invoking grep without a shell using child_process.spawnSync or execFileSync (e.g., call 'grep' with args array instead of interpolating into a template string), pass the keyword as a separate argument and add --fixed-strings (or -F) so grep treats the keyword literally, keep cwd as this.workingDir and existing options (encoding, timeout, stdio), and continue using matchingFiles.add(join(this.workingDir, ...)) for results; remove the custom double-quote escaping and the string interpolation to eliminate shell interpretation. In `@src/services/openai-service.ts`: - Around line 153-204: The PromptRewriter pipeline never uses the implemented extractResearchQuestions method—update PromptRewriter.rewrite to call and await this.extractResearchQuestions(ticket) first, then pass the resulting questions into the missing conductObjectiveResearch(questions, codeContext) step (implement or call the existing method), and finally call rewritePrompt(ticket, research) with the research results; ensure you await each async call, handle errors/fallbacks (e.g., default empty research or questions) and propagate or log errors consistently so the 3-step flow (extractResearchQuestions → conductObjectiveResearch → rewritePrompt) is executed in order. - Around line 196-199: The current post-processing on the LLM output uses content.split(...).map(...).filter(q => q.length > 10) which may drop valid short questions; change the filter to a lower threshold (e.g., q.length > 5) or remove it entirely so short but valid items like "Where is auth?" are kept—update the .filter(...) predicate in the same chain that follows content.split and .map to use the new threshold. - Around line 90-151: The rewritePrompt implementation breaks the intended 3-step pipeline: update the signature to async rewritePrompt(originalTicket: string, objectiveResearch: string): Promise, implement extractResearchQuestions(originalTicket: string): Promise and conductObjectiveResearch(questions: string[]): Promise (or similar names used in the spec), and change callers to call extractResearchQuestions -> conductObjectiveResearch -> rewritePrompt, passing the produced objectiveResearch into rewritePrompt; inside rewritePrompt incorporate the objectiveResearch into the prompt messages (e.g., append it to systemPrompt or include a dedicated assistant/user message) so the model uses the research when generating the rewritten prompt and keep existing error handling behavior. In `@thoughts/shared/specs/2026-03-05-prompt-rewriting.md`: - Around line 77-96: The markdown file has fenced code blocks starting with "```markdown" containing the sections like "## Task", "## Current Behavior", etc., but they are not separated by blank lines which causes rendering issues; update each fenced code block (the ones containing the headings "## Task", "## Current Behavior", "## Expected Behavior", "## Relevant Files", "## Acceptance Criteria", "## Constraints") to ensure there is a blank line before and after the opening and closing ``` markers so each code block is surrounded by a blank line for proper Markdown rendering. - Around line 42-47: The implementation is missing the objective research step: implement the conductObjectiveResearch(questions: string[]) method in openai-service.ts to run a fresh LLM/search over the codebase and return structured factual findings, then update prompt-rewriter.ts to call conductObjectiveResearch(extractedQuestions) before invoking rewritePrompt and change rewritePrompt(originalTicket) to rewritePrompt(originalTicket, researchFindings) so the rewriter receives the research results; ensure the place that references conductObjectiveResearch() uses the new implementation and adjust any tests or call sites to pass and consume the research findings. --- Outside diff comments: In `@src/services/claude-cli-executor.ts`: - Around line 237-242: generateTaskBreakdown currently passes the prompt string in the args array (the args variable containing '--print', '--model', '--max-turns', prompt) which is inconsistent with executeTask that writes the prompt to stdin to avoid CLI-escaping issues; title: Feature/prompt rewriting
content:
PR #15: Feature/prompt rewriting

Adds an optional `--rewrite-prompt` step that cleans up noisy tickets (Slack threads, metadata, boilerplate) before sending them to Claude Code. GPT-4o-mini reformats the ticket into a structured prompt while the original is stored in the DB. Also fixes a CLI bug where `--disallowed-tools` could accidentally consume the task prompt during argument parsing.


## Verify

  Build: `npm run build` `npm link`

  1. `ivan --rewrite-prompt "Add a loading spinner"` → see "🔄 Rewriting prompt..." before Claude
  runs
  2. `ivan "some task" --rewrite-prompt` → same, flag order shouldn't matter
  3. `ivan --rewrite-prompt` → prompts interactively, doesn't crash
  4. `ivan "some task"` → no rewriting, unchanged behaviour

  **Verify DB after any rewrite run:**
  `sqlite3 ~/.ivan/db.sqlite "SELECT description, original_description FROM tasks ORDER BY uuid DESC
  LIMIT 1;"`
  `description` = rewritten · `original_description` = raw input

Changed files:
- src/database/migrations/014_add_original_description_column.ts
- src/database/migrations/index.ts
- src/database/types.ts
- src/index.ts
- src/services/claude-cli-executor.ts
- src/services/job-manager.ts
- src/services/openai-service.ts
- src/services/prompt-rewriter.ts
- src/services/task-executor.ts
- src/types/non-interactive-config.ts

title: PR issue comment by mchlggr
content:
Before Rewrite:  
```
Context from Slack #ari-reliability:

When a user clicks a magic link from SciSummary Slack to onboard Ari, if they are already logged into a different Ari account in the browser (e.g., Ariso org), the existing session persists
Result: they see an old/incorrect onboarding or profile page tied to the existing session instead of the intended workspace/account from the magic link
Current workaround: manually visit web.ari.ariso.ai/my/profile, log out, then re-click the Slack link
Impact:

Confusing initial experience, especially for users who belong to multiple orgs or have prior sessions
Makes it look like Ari is “stuck” or broken when connecting Slack
Expected behavior:

Clicking a magic link from Slack should either:
Establish a session for the intended account/workspace directly, OR
Explicitly prompt the user to switch accounts / confirm which workspace to use
Acceptance criteria:

Repro steps documented
Magic link flow updated so that clicking from Slack correctly associates the session with the intended account/workspace, regardless of prior session
Add tests for magic-link + existing-session behavior

From Slack: Max requested a build trigger comment once created: “@ivan-agent /build”
```

After Rewrite: 
```
## Task
Fix the SciSummary Slack magic-link onboarding flow so that clicking the link correctly establishes/associates the browser session with the intended Ari account/workspace even when the user already has an active session for a different Ari account.

## Current Behavior
If a user clicks a magic link from SciSummary Slack to onboard Ari while already logged into a different Ari account in the same browser (e.g., Ariso org), the existing session persists and the user is shown an old/incorrect onboarding or profile page tied to the existing session instead of the intended workspace/account from the magic link.

## Expected Behavior
Clicking a magic link from Slack should either:
- Establish a session for the intended account/workspace directly, **or**
- Explicitly prompt the user to switch accounts / confirm which workspace to use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reproduction steps for the reported issue are documented.
- The Slack magic-link flow is updated so that clicking from Slack correctly associates the session with the intended account/workspace, regardless of any prior existing session in the browser.
- Tests are added to cover the “magic link + existing session for a different account” behavior.

## Open Questions for Research
- Where the SciSummary Slack magic link is generated and what parameters it includes to identify the intended account/workspace.
- Where in the web app the magic link is handled (route/controller/middleware) and how it currently interacts with existing sessions.
- What the current onboarding/profile routing logic is that causes the user to land on the “old/incorrect onboarding or profile page.”
- What test framework and existing coverage exist for magic-link authentication/onboarding flows, and where to add the new tests.

## Agent Instructions
Answer all open questions yourself using the codebase and search — do not ask the user. Make your best judgment based on available context and proceed with implementation. We are a pre-revenue startup. Avoid unnecessary complexity. Is this implementation overly complex? Are we introducing abstractions too early? Can we ship a simpler version? Prefer editing existing patterns over introducing new abstractions. **Do NOT stop until all affected tests, lints, builds and typechecks pass.**
```

title: Review thread on src/services/openai-service.ts:null
content:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**`extractResearchQuestions` is implemented but never consumed.**

This method exists and works, but the pipeline orchestration in `PromptRewriter` (per context snippet 2, line 20) never calls it. The current flow just calls `rewritePrompt(originalTicket)` directly.

For the 3-step pipeline to work as designed, `PromptRewriter.rewrite()` needs to:
1. Call `extractResearchQuestions(ticket)` 
2. Call the missing `conductObjectiveResearch(questions, codeContext)`
3. Call `rewritePrompt(ticket, research)`

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/services/openai-service.ts` around lines 153 - 204, The PromptRewriter
pipeline never uses the implemented extractResearchQuestions method—update
PromptRewriter.rewrite to call and await this.extractResearchQuestions(ticket)
first, then pass the resulting questions into the missing
conductObjectiveResearch(questions, codeContext) step (implement or call the
existing method), and finally call rewritePrompt(ticket, research) with the
research results; ensure you await each async call, handle errors/fallbacks
(e.g., default empty research or questions) and propagate or log errors
consistently so the 3-step flow (extractResearchQuestions →
conductObjectiveResearch → rewritePrompt) is executed in order.
```

</details>

content:
♻️ Duplicate comments (1)
src/services/openai-service.ts (1)
`93-95`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **Keep initialization inside the guarded block.** `ensureInitialized()` still runs before the `try`, so `promptForMissingConfig('openaiApiKey')` can make this optional rewrite path interactive and bypass the `Prompt rewrite failed:` wrapper. That undermines the graceful fallback the callers expect in `src/services/task-executor.ts:144-157` and `src/services/task-executor.ts:746-759`.
Suggested fix ```diff async rewritePrompt(ticket: string): Promise { - await this.ensureInitialized(); - const systemPrompt = `You are a prompt optimizer for coding agents. Transform noisy development tickets into concise, execution-ready prompts in markdown. @@ try { + const config = this.configManager.getConfig(); + if (!config?.openaiApiKey) { + throw new Error('OpenAI API key is required when prompt rewriting is enabled'); + } + + await this.ensureInitialized(); if (!this.openai) throw new Error('OpenAI client not initialized'); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/services/openai-service.ts` around lines 93 - 95, In rewritePrompt (async rewritePrompt(ticket: string): Promise) move the await this.ensureInitialized() call inside the try block so initialization (and any interactive promptForMissingConfig('openaiApiKey')) is guarded by the same try/catch that wraps the rewrite logic; this ensures any failures are caught and produce the expected graceful fallback (matching callers in task-executor.ts), i.e., call ensureInitialized() after entering try and before any API/interactive calls so the catch can wrap and log "Prompt rewrite failed:" as intended. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Duplicate comments: In `@src/services/openai-service.ts`: - Around line 93-95: In rewritePrompt (async rewritePrompt(ticket: string): Promise) move the await this.ensureInitialized() call inside the try block so initialization (and any interactive promptForMissingConfig('openaiApiKey')) is guarded by the same try/catch that wraps the rewrite logic; this ensures any failures are caught and produce the expected graceful fallback (matching callers in task-executor.ts), i.e., call ensureInitialized() after entering try and before any API/interactive calls so the catch can wrap and log "Prompt rewrite failed:" as intended. ```

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Verify that conductObjectiveResearch is not implemented anywhere
echo "=== Searching for conductObjectiveResearch implementation ==="
rg -n 'conductObjectiveResearch' --type ts

echo ""
echo "=== Searching for how rewritePrompt is called ==="
rg -n -A3 'rewritePrompt\(' --type ts

echo ""
echo "=== Check the full openai-service.ts file to understand the implementation ==="
wc -l src/services/openai-service.ts
```

Repository: ariso-ai/ivan

Length of output: 1027

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Check the prompt-rewriter.ts file to understand the full pipeline orchestration
echo "=== Full content of prompt-rewriter.ts ==="
cat src/services/prompt-rewriter.ts

echo ""
echo "=== Check if extractResearchQuestions exists ==="
rg -n 'extractResearchQuestions' --type ts
```

Repository: ariso-ai/ivan

Length of output: 894

---



</details>

**Critical: `rewritePrompt` implementation doesn't match the 3-step pipeline design.**

The method signature is missing the `objectiveResearch` parameter. According to the implementation plan, it should be:
```typescript
async rewritePrompt(originalTicket: string, objectiveResearch: string): Promise<string>
```

But the actual implementation only accepts:
```typescript
async rewritePrompt(ticket: string): Promise<string>
```

This breaks the intended 3-step pipeline. The two upstream steps—`extractResearchQuestions` and `conductObjectiveResearch`—are completely missing from the codebase. The result is a simple 1-step rewrite that ignores the research phase designed to prevent "intent leakage" as described in the spec. The prompt-rewriter orchestration just calls `rewritePrompt` directly with the original ticket, bypassing the entire research layer.

Both `extractResearchQuestions()` and `conductObjectiveResearch()` must be implemented, and `rewritePrompt` must accept and incorporate the objective research into the rewrite prompt.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/services/openai-service.ts` around lines 90 - 151, The rewritePrompt
implementation breaks the intended 3-step pipeline: update the signature to
async rewritePrompt(originalTicket: string, objectiveResearch: string):
Promise<string>, implement extractResearchQuestions(originalTicket: string):
Promise<string[]> and conductObjectiveResearch(questions: string[]):
Promise<string> (or similar names used in the spec), and change callers to call
extractResearchQuestions -> conductObjectiveResearch -> rewritePrompt, passing
the produced objectiveResearch into rewritePrompt; inside rewritePrompt
incorporate the objectiveResearch into the prompt messages (e.g., append it to
systemPrompt or include a dedicated assistant/user message) so the model uses
the research when generating the rewritten prompt and keep existing error
handling behavior.
```

</details>

title: Review COMMENTED by coderabbitai
content:
**Actionable comments posted: 2** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> src/services/task-executor.ts (1)
> > `743-772`: _🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_ > > **Inconsistent original description storage pattern and repeated instantiation.** > > Two issues: > > 1. **`PromptRewriter` is instantiated inside the loop** (line 752) - same inefficiency as the interactive flow. Create it once before the loop. > > 2. **Inconsistent storage pattern**: The interactive flow (lines 151-152) always stores `result.original` via `updateTaskOriginalDescription`, whereas here (lines 764-766) you check `if (description !== taskDescription)` first. While functionally equivalent (since `result.original === taskDescription`), the patterns differ and could diverge if the rewriter changes. > > > >
> ♻️ Proposed fix > > ```diff > // Optionally rewrite prompts using 3-step pipeline > const shouldRewrite = config.rewritePrompt || false; > + const rewriter = shouldRewrite ? new PromptRewriter(this.getOpenAIService(), true) : null; > > for (const taskDescription of finalTasks) { > let description = taskDescription; > + let originalDescription: string | null = null; > > - if (shouldRewrite) { > + if (shouldRewrite && rewriter) { > try { > console.log(chalk.blue('🔄 Rewriting prompt...')); > - const rewriter = new PromptRewriter(this.getOpenAIService(), true); > const result = await rewriter.rewrite(taskDescription); > description = result.rewritten; > + originalDescription = result.original; > console.log(chalk.green('✅ Prompt rewritten')); > } catch (error) { > console.warn(chalk.yellow('⚠️ Prompt rewriting failed, using original:'), error); > } > } > > const taskUuid = await this.jobManager.createTask(jobUuid, description, repository.id, 'build'); > > // Store the original ticket text if rewriting changed the description > - if (description !== taskDescription) { > - await this.jobManager.updateTaskOriginalDescription(taskUuid, taskDescription); > + if (originalDescription) { > + await this.jobManager.updateTaskOriginalDescription(taskUuid, originalDescription); > } > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@src/services/task-executor.ts` around lines 743 - 772, Move PromptRewriter > creation out of the loop (create a single instance before iterating finalTasks) > to avoid repeated instantiation; when shouldRewrite is true call > rewriter.rewrite(taskDescription) and capture both result.rewritten and > result.original, then always pass result.original to > jobManager.updateTaskOriginalDescription(taskUuid, result.original) after > creating the task (do not conditionalize on description !== taskDescription) so > the storage pattern matches the interactive flow; ensure you still fall back to > taskDescription when rewrite fails and keep using jobManager.createTask and > jobManager.getTask as before. > ``` > >
> >
> >
♻️ Duplicate comments (2)
src/services/openai-service.ts (1)
`93-94`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **`ensureInitialized()` before `try` block can trigger interactive prompts in non-interactive context.** The call to `ensureInitialized()` at line 94 happens outside the `try` block and can invoke `promptForMissingConfig('openaiApiKey')` (see lines 17-21), making `--rewrite-prompt` unexpectedly interactive when used in `executeNonInteractiveWorkflow`. Move the initialization inside the `try` block and fail fast if the API key is missing.
🔧 Proposed fix ```diff async rewritePrompt(ticket: string): Promise { - await this.ensureInitialized(); - const systemPrompt = `You are a prompt optimizer...`; const MAX_TICKET_LENGTH = 24000; // ... truncation logic ... try { + const config = this.configManager.getConfig(); + if (!config?.openaiApiKey) { + throw new Error('OpenAI API key is required when prompt rewriting is enabled'); + } + await this.ensureInitialized(); if (!this.openai) throw new Error('OpenAI client not initialized'); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/services/openai-service.ts` around lines 93 - 94, The call to ensureInitialized() in rewritePrompt is currently outside the try block and can trigger interactive prompts (via promptForMissingConfig) in non-interactive flows; move the ensureInitialized() invocation inside the existing try in rewritePrompt, and add a fast-fail check that throws or returns an error if the OpenAI API key is missing after initialization (so executeNonInteractiveWorkflow won't become interactive). Update references in rewritePrompt and any related error handling to rely on the moved ensureInitialized() and explicitly check for the api key presence before any prompting logic. ```
src/index.ts (1)
`601-602`: _🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_ **Filter only excludes `--` flags, not single-dash flags.** The filter `!arg.startsWith('--')` would let single-dash flags like `-c` pass through as potential task descriptions. While unlikely to cause issues with current usage, this could break if single-letter flags are added later.
♻️ More robust flag filtering ```diff - const nonFlagArgs = args.filter(arg => !arg.startsWith('--')); + const nonFlagArgs = args.filter(arg => !arg.startsWith('-')); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/index.ts` around lines 601 - 602, The current args filtering (nonFlagArgs = args.filter(arg => !arg.startsWith('--'))) only excludes double-dash flags and will let single-dash flags (e.g., -c) be treated as task text; change the filter to exclude any argument that begins with a dash so single-letter flags are also filtered out (update the args.filter usage that creates nonFlagArgs and keep hasRewriteFlag = args.includes('--rewrite-prompt') as-is or adjust flag checks consistently), e.g., replace the predicate with one that tests for a leading '-' (or a regex like /^-/) so both '-' and '--' style flags are ignored. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Inline comments: In `@src/database/migrations/014_add_prompt_rewriting_columns.ts`: - Around line 1-12: The migration file name is inconsistent with the migration content and name: update the filename to match the migration.name and the actual change (rename the current file to 014_add_original_description_column.ts) so the filename reflects that this migration adds the original_description column; ensure the exported const migration (id: 14, name: 'add_original_description_column', and the up/down SQL statements adding/dropping original_description) remain unchanged. In `@src/services/task-executor.ts`: - Around line 144-157: Move PromptRewriter instantiation out of the per-task loop to avoid recreating a stateless instance for every task: when rewritePrompt is true, create a single const rewriter = new PromptRewriter(this.getOpenAIService()) before iterating over buildTasks, then inside the for loop call rewriter.rewrite(task.description) and proceed to await this.jobManager.updateTaskOriginalDescription(task.uuid, result.original) and assign task.description = result.rewritten; keep the existing try/catch behavior but remove the in-loop new PromptRewriter(...) call. --- Outside diff comments: In `@src/services/task-executor.ts`: - Around line 743-772: Move PromptRewriter creation out of the loop (create a single instance before iterating finalTasks) to avoid repeated instantiation; when shouldRewrite is true call rewriter.rewrite(taskDescription) and capture both result.rewritten and result.original, then always pass result.original to jobManager.updateTaskOriginalDescription(taskUuid, result.original) after creating the task (do not conditionalize on description !== taskDescription) so the storage pattern matches the interactive flow; ensure you still fall back to taskDescription when rewrite fails and keep using jobManager.createTask and jobManager.getTask as before. --- Duplicate comments: In `@src/index.ts`: - Around line 601-602: The current args filtering (nonFlagArgs = args.filter(arg => !arg.startsWith('--'))) only excludes double-dash flags and will let single-dash flags (e.g., -c) be treated as task text; change the filter to exclude any argument that begins with a dash so single-letter flags are also filtered out (update the args.filter usage that creates nonFlagArgs and keep hasRewriteFlag = args.includes('--rewrite-prompt') as-is or adjust flag checks consistently), e.g., replace the predicate with one that tests for a leading '-' (or a regex like /^-/) so both '-' and '--' style flags are ignored. In `@src/services/openai-service.ts`: - Around line 93-94: The call to ensureInitialized() in rewritePrompt is currently outside the try block and can trigger interactive prompts (via promptForMissingConfig) in non-interactive flows; move the ensureInitialized() invocation inside the existing try in rewritePrompt, and add a fast-fail check that throws or returns an error if the OpenAI API key is missing after initialization (so executeNonInteractiveWorkflow won't become interactive). Update references in rewritePrompt and any related error handling to rely on the moved ensureInitialized() and explicitly check for the api key presence before any prompting logic. ```
---

src/services/openai-service.ts (2)
`151-157`: **LGTM!** The ticket truncation logic correctly bounds input size and preserves both head and tail context, addressing the concern about unbounded tickets hitting model limits. --- `159-179`: **LGTM!** The API call configuration and error handling are appropriate. The error wrapping provides clear context for failures.
